  • directives — Application directives »

geography¶

The geography directive describes the projection required and aggregates different attributes. The keywords in geography match mostly the definition of the method basemap in matplotlib. Other attributes match different function of the basemap module: coastlines, continents, boundary, countries, states, rivers, parallels, meridians. http://matplotlib.github.com/basemap/api/basemap_api.html?highlight=continents#mpl_toolkits.basemap.Basemap

    unique True

west None

    unique True
       
zorder 1000    
    unique True

east¶

default: None

The coordinates of the geography are normally inherited from the domain name specified, unless overwritten here by the user

attribute value
optional True
type float
unique True

fix_aspect¶

default: None

attribute value
optional True
unique True

hemisphere¶

default: None

for some projections such as stereograhic or orthographic, you can specify a hemisphere

attribute value
optional True
unique True
validate ValidateChoice

attribute value
optional True

parallels¶

default: “”

attribute value
type parallels
unique True

plot_below¶

default: continents

list here the attributes you want to have plotted below the data, if an attribute is not listed, it is not plotted

attribute value
validate ValidateChoice
  coastlines
  continents
  boundary
  countries
  states
  rivers
  parallels
  meridians

plot_over¶

default: coastlines

list here the attribute you want to have plotted over the data, if an attribute is not listed, it is not plotted

attribute value
validate ValidateChoice
  coastlines
  continents
  boundary
  countries
  states
  rivers
  parallels
  meridians

resolution¶

default: c

The resolution of the boundary database to use: c for crude, l for low, i for intermediate, h for high, f for full.

attribute value
unique True
validate ValidateChoice
  c
  l
  i
  h
  f
